title: Orlando bus routes — airport / mall / Gatorland directions
html:
TL;DR

  • 17yo Yahir navigates Orlando entirely by public bus during a March 2021 trip — Westlake 42 to airport, route 108 to Cypress Crossing in front of Gatorland, 30-minute transfer waits — captured as a self-addressed directions note left in
  • The stop "across the street in front BJ's" anchors the routing, suggesting the trip's hotel or pickup was on International Drive — the Orlando tourist corridor
  • Earliest documented instance of the scrappy travel pattern that becomes a Yahir-signature by 2025 (2025 05 20 side quests list "Patagonia with Huck", 2025 05 20 scrappy flight 400 round trip)
  • Same Orlando-March-2021 trip is referenced in the March 13 booking note — this is the directions-leg of that itinerary
  • 6:11 AM bus to airport implies an early-morning return flight; the note functions as a what-time-do-I-leave self-brief

Notes

A throwaway logistics scratch — the kind of note that vanishes from memory but is exactly what the preserves. The substance is procedural (Westlake 42 bus, route 108, Florida Mall transfer, "1:47 hours" total trip time). The interest is biographical: a 17-year-old small town Oregon kid is doing solo public-transit Orlando, six months before he leaves for OSU. The pattern that becomes scrappy travel — refuse the rideshare premium, take the public option, eat the time cost — starts here, in a peak COVID solo trip pre-vaccination.

The references to "BJ's" and "Gatorland" suggest at least one tourism-checkbox stop. The trip likely connects to the broader 2021 push toward independence and travel that culminates in the 18 year old fly away parable his dad tells him weeks later.

Connections

orlando fl · gatorland · bjs wholesale · scrappy travel · 2021 03 13 orlando tickets and hotel
